lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਥਾਇ ਸਾਖੀ ਮਹਾ ਪੁਰਖ ਬੋਲਦੇ ਸਾਝੀ ਸਗਲ ਜਹਾਨੈ ॥
lines.translit: parathaae saakhee mahaa purakh bolade saajhee sagal jahaanai |
lines.english: Great men speak the teachings by relating them to individual situations, but the whole world shares in them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਜੀਵਤੁ ਮਰੈ ਤਾ ਮਨ ਹੀ ਤੇ ਮਨੁ ਮਾਨੈ ॥
lines.translit: gur parasaadee jeevat marai taa man hee te man maanai |
lines.english: If, by Guru's Grace, one remains dead while yet alive, the mind becomes content in itself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਇ ਸੁ ਭਉ ਕਰੇ ਆਪਣਾ ਆਪੁ ਪਛਾਣੈ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh hoe su bhau kare aapanaa aap pachhaanai |
lines.english: One who becomes Gurmukh knows the Fear of God, and realizes his own self.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਚਿਤੁ ਨ ਲਾਇਓ ਅੰਤਿ ਦੁਖੁ ਪਹੁਤਾ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh chit na laaeo ant dukh pahutaa aae |
lines.english: Those who do not focus their consciousness on the Lord, as Gurmukh, suffer pain and grief in the end.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਕਉ ਮਨ ਕੀ ਪਰਤੀਤਿ ਨਾਹੀ ਨਾਨਕ ਸੇ ਕਿਆ ਕਥਹਿ ਗਿਆਨੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jin kau man kee parateet naahee naanak se kiaa katheh giaanai |1|
lines.english: Those who have no faith in their own minds, O Nanak - how can they speak of spiritual wisdom? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਦਰਹੁ ਬਾਹਰਹੁ ਅੰਧਿਆਂ ਸੁਧਿ ਨ ਕਾਈ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: andarahu baaharahu andhiaan sudh na kaaee paae |
lines.english: They are blind, inwardly and outwardly, and they do not understand anything.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਡਿਤ ਤਿਨ ਕੀ ਬਰਕਤੀ ਸਭੁ ਜਗਤੁ ਖਾਇ ਜੋ ਰਤੇ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: panddit tin kee barakatee sabh jagat khaae jo rate har naae |
lines.english: O Pandit, O religious scholar, the whole world is fed for the sake of those who are attuned to the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਸਲਾਹਿਆ ਹਰਿ ਸਿਉ ਰਹੇ ਸਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jin gur kai sabad salaahiaa har siau rahe samaae |
lines.english: Those who praise the Word of the Guru's Shabad, remain blended with the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਡਿਤ ਦੂਜੈ ਭਾਇ ਬਰਕਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਵਈ ਨਾ ਧਨੁ ਪਲੈ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: panddit doojai bhaae barakat na hovee naa dhan palai paae |
lines.english: O Pandit, O religious scholar, no one is satisfied, and no one finds true wealth through the love of duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜਿ ਥਕੇ ਸੰਤੋਖੁ ਨ ਆਇਓ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਜਲਤ ਵਿਹਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: parr thake santokh na aaeo anadin jalat vihaae |
lines.english: They have grown weary of reading scriptures, but still, they do not find contentment, and they pass their lives burning, night and day.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੂਕ ਪੂਕਾਰ ਨ ਚੁਕਈ ਨਾ ਸੰਸਾ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: kook pookaar na chukee naa sansaa vichahu jaae |
lines.english: Their cries and complaints never end, and doubt does not depart from within them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮ ਵਿਹੂਣਿਆ ਮੁਹਿ ਕਾਲੈ ਉਠਿ ਜਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak naam vihooniaa muhi kaalai utth jaae |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, without the Naam, the Name of the Lord, they rise up and depart with blackened faces. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਹਰਿ ਦਸੇ ਮਿਤੁ ਤਿਸੁ ਹਉ ਬਲਿ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: jo har dase mit tis hau bal jaaee |
lines.english: I am a sacrifice to that Friend, who shows it to me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸਜਣ ਮੇਲਿ ਪਿਆਰੇ ਮਿਲਿ ਪੰਥੁ ਦਸਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: har sajan mel piaare mil panth dasaaee |
lines.english: O Beloved, lead me to meet my True Friend; meeting with Him, I shall ask Him to show me the Path.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਲਿਹਾਰੀ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਤਿਸੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਸੋਝੀ ਪਾਈ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: balihaaree satigur tis jin sojhee paaee |12|
lines.english: I am a sacrifice to the True Guru, who has imparted this understanding to me. ||12||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਣ ਸਾਝੀ ਤਿਨ ਸਿਉ ਕਰੀ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: gun saajhee tin siau karee har naam dhiaaee |
lines.english: I share His Virtues with Him, and meditate on the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸੇਵੀ ਪਿਆਰਾ ਨਿਤ ਸੇਵਿ ਹਰਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: har sevee piaaraa nit sev har sukh paaee |
lines.english: I serve my Beloved Lord forever; serving the Lord, I have found peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਡਿਤ ਮੈਲੁ ਨ ਚੁਕਈ ਜੇ ਵੇਦ ਪੜੈ ਜੁਗ ਚਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: panddit mail na chukee je ved parrai jug chaar |
lines.english: O Pandit, O religious scholar, your filth shall not be erased, even if you read the Vedas for four ages.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਭੁਖ ਹੈ ਮੂਰਖ ਭੁਖਿਆ ਮੁਏ ਗਵਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: antar trisanaa bhukh hai moorakh bhukhiaa mue gavaar |
lines.english: They are filled with thirst and hunger; the ignorant fools starve to death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਡਿਤ ਭੂਲੇ ਦੂਜੈ ਲਾਗੇ ਮਾਇਆ ਕੈ ਵਾਪਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: panddit bhoole doojai laage maaeaa kai vaapaar |
lines.english: The Pandits are deluded, attached to duality, and they deal only in Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤ੍ਰੈ ਗੁਣ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੂਲੁ ਹੈ ਵਿਚਿ ਹਉਮੈ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: trai gun maaeaa mool hai vich haumai naam visaar |
lines.english: The three qualities are the roots of Maya; in egotism, one forgets the Naam, the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਦਰਹੁ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਭੁਖ ਗਈ ਸਚੈ ਨਾਇ ਪਿਆਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: andarahu trisanaa bhukh gee sachai naae piaar |
lines.english: Hunger and thirst have departed from within me; I am in love with the True Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਸੇਵਿਐ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਸਚੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਵੀਚਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: satigur seviai sukh paaeaa sachai sabad veechaar |
lines.english: Serving the True Guru, peace is obtained, contemplating the True Word of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮਿ ਰਤੇ ਸਹਜੇ ਰਜੇ ਜਿਨਾ ਹਰਿ ਰਖਿਆ ਉਰਿ ਧਾਰਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak naam rate sahaje raje jinaa har rakhiaa ur dhaar |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, those who are imbued with the Naam, who keep the Lord clasped tightly to their hearts, are automatically satisfied. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਸੇਵਿਆ ਦੁਖੁ ਲਗਾ ਬਹੁਤਾ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh har naam na seviaa dukh lagaa bahutaa aae |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh does not serve the Lord's Name, and so he suffers in horrible pain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਅਗਿਆਨੁ ਅੰਧੇਰੁ ਹੈ ਸੁਧਿ ਨ ਕਾਈ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: antar agiaan andher hai sudh na kaaee paae |
lines.english: He is filled with the darkness of ignorance, and he does not understand anything.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਨਿਧਾਨੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਿਆ ਦੂਜੈ ਲਗਾ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: naam nidhaan visaariaa doojai lagaa jaae |
lines.english: He has forgotten the treasure of the Naam; he is caught in the love of duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਹਠਿ ਸਹਜਿ ਨ ਬੀਜਿਓ ਭੁਖਾ ਕਿ ਅਗੈ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: manahatth sehaj na beejio bhukhaa ki agai khaae |
lines.english: Because of his stubborn mind, he does not plant the seeds of intuitive peace; what will he eat in the world hereafter, to satisfy his hunger?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਿਲਹਿ ਵਡਿਆਈਆ ਜੇ ਆਪੇ ਮੇਲਿ ਮਿਲਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh mileh vaddiaaeea je aape mel milaae |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukhs are honored with glory, when the Lord Himself unites them in His Union. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਰਸਨਾ ਹਰਿ ਜਸੁ ਗਾਵੈ ਖਰੀ ਸੁਹਾਵਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: har rasanaa har jas gaavai kharee suhaavanee |
lines.english: The tongue which sings the Lord's Praises, is so very beautiful.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਮਨਿ ਤਨਿ ਮੁਖਿ ਹਰਿ ਬੋਲੈ ਸਾ ਹਰਿ ਭਾਵਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: jo man tan mukh har bolai saa har bhaavanee |
lines.english: One who speaks the Lord's Name, with mind, body and mouth, is pleasing to the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਚਖੈ ਸਾਦੁ ਸਾ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਾਵਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: jo guramukh chakhai saad saa tripataavanee |
lines.english: That Gurmukh tastes the the sublime taste of the Lord, and is satisfied.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵੈ ਪਿਆਰੇ ਨਿਤ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਇ ਗੁਣੀ ਸਮਝਾਵਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: gun gaavai piaare nit gun gaae gunee samajhaavanee |
lines.english: She sings continually the Glorious Praises of her Beloved; singing His Glorious Praises, she is uplifted.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਆਪਿ ਦਇਆਲੁ ਸਾ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੂ ਗੁਰੂ ਬੁਲਾਵਣੀ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: jis hovai aap deaal saa satiguroo guroo bulaavanee |13|
lines.english: She is blessed with the Lord's Mercy, and she chants the Words of the Guru, the True Guru. ||13||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਤਨੁ ਆਗੈ ਰਾਖਿ ਕੈ ਊਭੀ ਸੇਵ ਕਰੇਇ ॥
lines.translit: man tan aagai raakh kai aoobhee sev karee |
lines.english: just so, we offer our minds and bodies to our Guru; we stand before Him, and serve Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਸਤੀ ਸਿਰਿ ਜਿਉ ਅੰਕਸੁ ਹੈ ਅਹਰਣਿ ਜਿਉ ਸਿਰੁ ਦੇਇ ॥
lines.translit: hasatee sir jiau ankas hai aharan jiau sir dee |
lines.english: The elephant offers its head to the reins, and the anvil offers itself to the hammer;
